channel.title: xNet Blog
channel.description: Essays on local-first software, data ownership, and the open web.

item.title: The Gentlest Furnace
item.description: A star carries the energy of a billion bombs and still feels calm from here. What hydrostatic equilibrium — the thermostat that keeps a star from exploding or going cold — teaches us about information, attention, and building technology that burns long instead of burning out.
item.author: xNet

item.title: Data Should Work Like Soil
item.description: Beneath every forest runs a fungal network — the original internet. What mycelium, the human nervous system, and Tesla’s Warp teach us about building one worth living in, and how to heal one that’s gone sick.
item.author: xNet

item.title: A Great Pirate Age for the Internet
item.description: What pirates — the real ones, and the ones in One Piece — can teach us about owning your data. An essay on freedom, self-governance, and why you are the cargo.
item.author: xNet
